name: Sulaymaniyah Museum tablet
title: Gilgamesh Tablet V — Cedar Forest
description: Old Babylonian tablet containing previously unknown lines of the Epic of Gilgamesh describing the journey through the Cedar Forest. Purchased by the Sulaymaniyah Museum in Iraq around 2011 and published in 2014. Includes a vivid description of the cedar trees and the monster Humbaba.
raw_atf:
&GILG5 = Sulaymaniyah Museum (Epic of Gilgamesh, Tablet V — Cedar Forest)
#atf: lang akk
#note: Old Babylonian. Acquired by Sulaymaniyah Museum (Iraq) ca. 2011, published 2014. Excerpt: the Cedar Forest and Humbaba.
@tablet
@obverse
1. er-s,e-tam ka-li-sza usz-ma-li a-bu-bu
2. i-na lib-bi qi-isz-tim sza ere-nim
3. ri-isz i-la-bi-ir-ma re-e-szu-um ka-li
4. it-ta-lak-ma Gi-il-ga-mesz iq-ta-bi
5. En-ki-du a-na qa-bi-su ur-ri-id-ma
6. a-na sa-ar sa-ri-im is-ta-na-as-szi
7. qi-isz-tum ra-pa-asz-tum u ma-le-at s,e-rum
8. ri-isz i-la-bi-ir it-ba-am a-bi
9. ma-la na-a-di-im s,e-rum be-e-lum
10. en-ma ri-isz-tam i-mu-ru us-sa-am-su-ni
11. i-na qi-isz-tim s,e-rum na-asz-pu-uh
12. i-na sa-ar qi-isz-tim u2 ri-isz-tam
13. er-s,e-tam ka-li-sza usz-ma-li a-bu-bu
14. ta-ab-ki qu-li ba-a-ri-im
15. li-ib-bi Hu-um-ba-ba szu-pu-u2
16. Hu-um-ba-ba sa da-an-nu ri-isz-su
17. si-it-nu-um szu-a-tum pa-la-asz
18. usz-szu-um la-a pa-la-asz-ma
19. ri-isz-tam pa-ni-szu i-na-an-na
20. na-ap-lu-us-szu-um-ma ia-a-szi-ma
@reverse
21. pa-ni Hu-um-ba-ba ki-i li-ib-bi-im
22. i-na li-ib-bi qi-isz-tim er-s,e-tim
23. it-ta-la-ku pa-ni-su-nu
24. er-s,e-tum it-ta-bu-uk dam-qa-am
25. pa-ni Hu-um-ba-ba u2-ul pa-ni a-wi-li-im
26. u2-ul pa-ni a-me-li-im szu-a-tu-ma
27. En-ki-du iq-ta-bi a-na Gi-il-ga-mesz
28. ib-bi-ma zu-um-ri-ka sza e-pe-ri-im
29. la ta-ap-pa-la-ah pa-na-a-a-am
30. la ta-na-ad-di pa-na-a-a-am
$ rest broken

sections.content: &GILG5 = Sulaymaniyah Museum (Epic of Gilgamesh, Tablet V — Cedar Forest)
#note: Old Babylonian. Acquired by Sulaymaniyah Museum (Iraq) ca. 2011, published 2014. Excerpt: the Cedar Forest and Humbaba.

sections.translation: The flood had filled the entire earth.
Inside the Cedar Forest
where the old trees rose and the crown of the forest spread,
Gilgamesh walked and spoke:
"Enkidu, I will go down ahead of you.
Call out to the wind of the south,
the forest is wide and full of serpents."
The forest was wide, full of creatures.
The crown rose up, the father of old arose,
all that there was in the cedar,
the creatures of the forest,
the wind of the cedar forest,
the flood had filled the whole earth,
weeping in silence from the wood,
the heart of Humbaba was aroused.
Humbaba, whose roar is the flood,
this battle is not a thing to fear,
the base is not a thing to fear.
Now his face is different,
look at him — he sees me —
the face of Humbaba is like the coils of intestines,
inside the cedar forest, the earth,
they went, their faces set forward.
The earth poured forth its good things.
The face of Humbaba is not a human face,
it is not the face of a man.
Enkidu spoke to Gilgamesh:
"Stand firm, do not fear,
do not turn your face away,
do not avert your gaze!"
